segments.text:
Homeostasis:
Maintenance of relatively constant internal physical and chemical conditions
Specific examples:
thermal regulation
blood glucose levels
blood Ca 2 + levels
Hormonal control of molting and metamorphosis

Endotherm
tolerates very narrow range of core body temperatures
maintains core temperature primarily by metabolic activity
Ectotherm
tolerates relatively wide range of core body temperatures
maintains core temperature primarily by external means
behavior, coloration
Heat exchange with the environment
Behavioral thermoregulation
Thermal regulation by color variation

26-28°C
29-33°C
33-38°C
38-44°C
human = 37°C below 35°C = hypothermia		below 32°C = death likely
above 38°C = hyperthermia 		above 42°C = death likely
Feedback loops
the human version

Cell signaling or cell-cell communication
hormone = chemical signal or signaling molecule

ALL except neurotransmitters
are considered “hormones”
difference is in relationship
between producing cells and
responding cells
Major types of hormones

Mechanism of action – steroid hormones
signal leads to changes in gene expression

water-soluble hormones
receptor protein in cell membrane
shape change
activates cytoplasmic protein
signal transduction cascade
cell response
shape change
metabolism change
change gene expression
Same hormone – different responses from different cell types

Same hormone – different responses from different cell types
same receptors
Same hormone – different responses from different cell types
